history.content:
<thought>
I am at row 02 column B facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 04 column D. Moving directly right is blocked by lava at row 02 column C, so I should head downward instead. Since I’m facing right, I’ll turn left to face up, then move down via turning right? Better: turn right from `>` to face down (`v`) and then move forward into row 03 column B, which is safe (`.`).
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 02 column B facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 04 column D. I need to go right toward the open corridor, so I should face right (`>`). From facing down, turning right (clockwise) will face left, so instead I turn left (counter-clockwise) to face right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 02 column B facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 04 column D. Lava blocks the direct route (row 02 column C is lava, and row 04 column C is lava), so I should move down the open column B: row 03 column B is `.`. Therefore I need to face down and move forward.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 02 column B facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 04 column D. From my current position, the safe path is to move down to row 03 column B (it is floor `.`), then continue toward column D while avoiding lava `L` at row 02 column C and row 04 column C. So the next step is to move forward one cell.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 03 column B facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 04 column D. From my current position, moving forward (to row 04 column B) is safe (it’s `.`), and it brings me closer horizontally/vertically toward the open path to the goal. So I’ll move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 04 column B facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 04 column D. I should move right toward the goal, but first I must avoid lava at row 04 column C (`L`). The safest route is to turn right to face left? Actually from B, facing right would go to C which is lava, and facing left would go to wall. Facing up would go to row 03 column B which is floor (`.`). So I will turn left to face up.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 04 column B facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 04 column D. I need to move forward toward column C, but column C (row 04 column C) is lava (`L`), which is deadly. So I should turn to avoid that path; turning left will face up, where row 03 column B is floor (`.`).
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 04 column B, facing up (`^`). The goal is at row 04 column D. Moving forward from here would go into a wall at row 03 column B, so I need to face right (`>`). From up (`^`) to right (`>`) is a TURN_RIGHT.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 04 column B facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 04 column D, so I need to move east. The cell at row 04 column C is lava (`L`), so I cannot go straight. I should turn to change direction to find a safe route around the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 04 column B facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 04 column D. I should move right, but currently I'm facing down, so I need to turn to face right (`>`). From down to right requires a 90° clockwise turn (TURN_RIGHT).
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 04 column B, facing left (`<`). The goal is at row 04 column D. From my position, column C is lava (`L`), so moving forward is unsafe. I should turn to face up (`^`) to look for a safe path around the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 04 column B, facing up (`^`). The goal is at row 04 column D, but column C at row 04 is lava (`L`), so I cannot move right directly. I should instead head upward to look for an alternate route around the lava by moving forward into row 03 column B (which is safe `.`).
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 03 column B, facing up (`^`). The goal is at row 04 column D. I should avoid the lava at row 02 column C and row 04 column C, and go toward the open corridor: from B facing up is blocked by a wall at row 02 column B, so first I’ll turn to the right (`>`).
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 03 column B facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 04 column D. From here, moving forward would go to row 03 column C, which is safe (`.`). To get to the goal, I should go right toward column D, avoiding lava at row 02 column C and row 04 column C. So I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 03 column C facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 04 column D. From here, moving forward would go to row 03 column D which is floor (`.`), not the goal. Moving forward to column D sets up getting down into the goal at row 04 column D next. That path avoids lava at row 04 column C (`L`) by approaching the goal from above.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
